edge_hypothesis: CCI Chop Fade v1 achieved PF=1.255 all-regime on ETH 4h — our first all-regime oscillator ACCEPT (Entry 004). Quandalf ordered a v3 at 8:1 R:R (Entry 009) because the 12:1 R:R may be too wide: many trades run to ~8-10 ATR profit then reverse back to exit at lower levels. v2 was accidentally identical to v1 (TP stayed at 18 ATR due to order error — Entry 005). THIS is the actual v3 test that was never executed. Hypothesis: tightening TP from 18 to 12 ATR (stop 1.5, giving 8:1 R:R) captures more winners at their peak before reversal, boosting win rate and PF past the 1.3 ACCEPT threshold. The Vortex family showed tighter geometry consistently improves PF (1.385 -> 1.892 across v1-v2c). An aggressive variant at 1.0 stop / 8.0 TP (also 8:1) tests the Supertrend sweet spot geometry on CCI signals.
target_regime: ranging (CHOP > 50 confirms range-bound market where CCI mean-reversion has proven PF=1.43) + transitional (CCI PF=1.52 in transitional regime)

variants.name: cci_chop_fade_v3_8to1
variants.description: CCI beyond +/-100 under CHOP ranging gate with 8:1 R:R (stop 1.5, TP 12). This is the ORDERED BUT NEVER EXECUTED v3. Direct iteration on v1 (PF=1.255 at 12:1). Only change: TP reduced from 18 to 12 ATR. Tests whether more trades hit TP before reversing.

components.notes: CCI_20_0.015 beyond +/-100 — continuous zone-based mean-reversion signal with proven 421-trade base
CHOP_14_1_100 > 50 confirms ranging market where mean-reversion has edge

variants.name: cci_chop_fade_v3_tight_8to1
variants.description: CCI beyond +/-100 under CHOP gate with AGGRESSIVE 8:1 geometry (stop 1.0, TP 8). Applies the Supertrend/Vortex lesson: tight stops cut loser damage, winners are captured by TP before reversal. Tests whether the tight-stop advantage discovered in Vortex (PF 1.385->1.892) transfers to CCI oscillator signals.

variants.name: cci_chop_fade_v3_deep_6to1
variants.description: CCI beyond +/-150 (deeper oversold/overbought) under CHOP gate with 6:1 R:R (stop 1.5, TP 9). Tests whether requiring DEEPER CCI extremes (-150/+150 vs -100/+100) produces higher-conviction mean-reversion entries. Fewer trades but each should be at a more extreme deviation from mean. Moderate R:R to match the shorter expected moves from extreme positions.

components.notes: CCI_20_0.015 beyond +/-150 — deep extreme zone for high-conviction mean-reversion
